title: elymus uralensis subsp. viridiglumis
intro: Elymus uralensis subsp. viridiglumis, also known as Ural wildrye or Ural wheat grass, is a subspecies of grass belonging to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Ural wildrye can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
